name: Vuelta a la Sierra de Almudaina — Circular desde Tollos
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Tollos are situated in the Comtat region of Alicante, Valencia, Spain, at an elevation of 780 meters above sea level. The area is characterized by a Mediterranean climate and features a landscape of hills and mountain ranges, such as the Sierra de Almudaina, offering varied terrain for cycling. This region provides a mix of challenging climbs and descents across its natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Tollos The most popular mountain bike trail is Els Arcs - Penya Rabosa — Circular desde Castell de Castells, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete, offering a circular route through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail, a difficult 27.1 miles (43.7 km) path. This route traverses a valley and varied terrain, providing a demanding experience. Local mountain bikers also love the Vuelta a la Sierra de Almudaina — Circular desde Tollos, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Almudaina mountain range, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Tollos is defined by mountain ranges, valleys, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Tollos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tollos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Tollos?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Tollos, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has explored these routes over 300 times, providing an average rating of 4.8 stars.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Tollos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tollos are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult. There are 8 moderate trails and 18 difficult trails, providing challenging climbs and descents across the region's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Tollos area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926077763/els-arcs-penya-rabosa-circular-desde-castell-de-castells" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Arcs - Penya Rabosa — Circular desde Castell de Castells&lt;/a&gt; trail is a moderate 14.8-mile (23.8 km) loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924813380/vuelta-a-la-sierra-de-almudaina-circular-desde-tollos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta a la Sierra de Almudaina — Circular desde Tollos&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.7-mile (34.9 km) moderate route through the Sierra de Almudaina.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tollos?
A: Tollos, situated at 780 meters above sea level, benefits from a Mediterranean climate with approximately 300 days of sunshine annually. This makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and generally dry conditions. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring cooler temperatures but still often allows for riding.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking around Tollos?
A: The region around Tollos is characterized by a landscape of hills and mountain ranges, such as the Sierra de Almudaina. You'll encounter varied terrain with challenging climbs and descents, traversing valleys and natural features typical of the Comtat region of Alicante.

Q: Are there any interesting natural landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area offers several natural landmarks and viewpoints. You might encounter sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views. The area also features unique formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/120805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Arches&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Tollos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tollos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the varied terrain, the challenging nature of the routes, and the scenic beauty of the mountain ranges and valleys.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926041074/vall-d-alcala-pla-de-petracos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.1-mile (43.7 km) path that traverses a valley and varied terrain, providing a demanding experience. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925790321/tour-por-las-comarcas-del-interior-alicantino-circular-desde-castell-de-castells" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour por las comarcas del interior alicantino — Circular desde Castell de Castells&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.9-mile (61.2 km) route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Tollos?
A: While specific cafes, pubs, or hotels directly on the trails are not detailed in the route information, Tollos is a municipality in the Comtat region. You can typically find local amenities, including places to eat and stay, within the village or in nearby towns in the Alicante province.

Q: What are some unique natural features to explore in the Tollos area?
A: Beyond the trails, the region boasts interesting natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de la Juvea&lt;/a&gt; cave and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4155453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/486065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Serpis River&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juvees d'Alt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer a mix of natural beauty and historical elements?
A: Many trails in the region will naturally pass through areas with historical significance or offer views of ancient structures. For instance, the general area includes sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/229747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam at Barranc del Infern&lt;/a&gt;, which can be found near some of the routes, blending natural beauty with historical engineering.
tips: Ruins in good condition located in a very beautiful environment. La Queirola is a farmhouse of Islamic origin of which there is already documentary evidence in the 13th century. After the conquest of the territory by the Conqueror King, it remained inhabited by 13 Moorish families who survived there in harsh conditions until their expulsion in 1602.

Site with incredible views towards the valleys (1010m asl). From here you can see the Benicadell and the Beniarrés reservoir. Access by mtb is somewhat complicated with steep sections and loose rock. In the end, the views more than make up for the effort to climb!

The central square of Millena is next to the Church and the town hall.

Enjoy this impressive elm tree that is over 300 years old.
